channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Extreme weather expected Saturday gets rarely used 'high risk' designation
item.description: A dynamic storm system threatening to spawn powerful tornadoes and hail as big as baseballs has earned a relatively rare designation from forecasters.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar thinks Selena 'partly to blame' for death: Report
item.description: Saldívar, 64, officially filed for parole in January. She shot Selena during a confrontation in a Texas hotel room.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Senate votes to avoid government shutdown
item.description: The Senate voted mostly along party lines Friday afternoon to pass the House Republican-drafted bill to fund government through September, avoiding a government shutdown only hours before funding was due to lapse. President Trump is expected to sign the bill into law. The final vote was 54-46.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Here's what you need to know about St. Patrick's Day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If it's March, and it's green, it must be St. Patrick's Day. The day honoring the patron saint of Ireland is a global celebration of Irish heritage.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Protestant denominations try new ideas as they face declines in members and money
item.description: When the Episcopal Church recently announced cuts to its national staff, it was the latest in a long-running cycle among historic U.S. Protestant denominations — declines in members leading to declines in funding and thus in staff. And it wasn't alone. The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Measles confirmed in 3 more states as cases multiply
item.description: More than 300 cases of measles, one of the most contagious viruses in the world, have now been confirmed across more than a dozen U.S. states, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: The government agency that runs the Voice of America has moved to terminate contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse, and told its journalists Friday to stop using material from the wire services.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge formally sentences Adnan Syed to time served in 'Serial' podcast case
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — A Maryland judge formally sentenced Adnan Syed on Friday to the time he's already served in prison, appearing to finally bring to a close a long-running case with numerous legal twists and turns that received worldwide attention from the true-crime podcast “Serial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma over immigration laws
item.description: The Trump administration has moved to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma for their immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Newborn kittens rescued from wrecked car moments before demolition
item.description: A litter of kittens was rescued from the inside of a wrecked car at a junkyard moments before it was scheduled to be demolished Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Johns Hopkins University cutting more than 2,000 workers due to federal aid cuts
item.description: “The termination of more than $800 million in [U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID)] funding is now forcing us to wind down critical work here in Baltimore and internationally,” a spokesperson for the school said in an email.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Arrest made after Austin, Texas 17-vehicle crash leaves 3 adults, 2 kids dead
item.description: Police in Austin, Texas arrested a suspect Friday following a 17-vehicle crash on I-35 that left five people dead, including a child and an infant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American Airlines Boeing 737 catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawsuit accuses man of killing, eating patients at mental health facility
item.description: The case against a South Carolina mental health facility where a man is accused of murdering and cannibalizing patients could be heading to trial.
item.dc:creator: Chase Laudenslager, KTLA 5

item.title: ICE Boston arrests Guatemalan national charged with child rape who was released back into community by court
item.description: Guatemalan national, Stivenson Omar Perez-Ajtzalan, 19, was arrested on Jan. 30 for aggravated rape of a child 10 years younger than him, according to ICE Boston.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US transfers immigrants out of Guantanamo Bay to Louisiana as court weighs legality
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. authorities have removed immigrants from detention facilities at the Guantanamo Bay naval base as a federal court in Washington weighs a challenge by civil rights advocates to holding immigrants at the offshore military station. A spokesperson for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 12 taken to hospital after fire seen under American Airlines plane at DIA
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Renowned sports writer dies suddenly at age 69
item.description: John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Megastorm may bring blizzards, tornadoes, flooding and even fires across much of US: Where, when it will hit
item.description: More than 100 million people in the U.S. will be in the path of an intense March storm starting Friday as the sprawling multi-day system threatens fires, blizzards, tornadoes, and flooding as it tracks eastward across the Great Plains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Laken Riley Act unleashes feds to hunt Venezuelan gang members in Florida: lawmaker
item.description: Homeland Security Investigations and Immigration and Customs Enforcement took suspected members of Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua into custody last week thanks to the newly enacted Laken Riley Act.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Farmers sue Trump administration over halted IRA grants
item.description: Farmers and environmental groups are suing the Trump administration over its decision to pause grants that are part of the Democrats’ climate, tax and healthcare law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to partly allow birthright citizenship restrictions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is asking the Supreme Court to allow restrictions on birthright citizenship to partly take effect while legal fights play out.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man describes cruelty during his two decades of captivity at his family home in Connecticut
item.description: A man rescued from a burning home in Connecticut told a shocking story of cruelty and constant hunger as he was held captive in a single room for 20 years by his father and stepmother, according to a newly released arrest warrant.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Connecticut woman arrested after stepson allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: A Connecticut woman accused of kidnapping a man and holding him captive for over 20 years was arrested Wednesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Lepore, ABC 4

item.title: Federal student loan site down Wednesday, a day after layoffs gutted Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An hours-long outage Wednesday on StudentAid.gov, the federal website for student loans and financial aid, underscored the risks in rapidly gutting the Department of Education, as President Donald Trump aims to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump must reinstate many fired federal employees
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that the Trump administration must reinstate probationary government employees fired unlawfully at several agencies, lambasting the Justice Department at a hearing for a “sham” gambit that enabled a key official to avoid testifying in the case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices, but the US is hesitant
item.description: Without a new policy including vaccines, the government will continue to slaughter every flock with a bird flu infection to limit the spread of the disease. Those deaths have totaled over 166 million birds in the U.S. since 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KRON4

item.title: Affordable housing threatened as Trump halts $1 billion slated for extending life of aging buildings
item.description: The Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing, threatening projects that keep tens of thousands of units livable for low-income Americans, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most AAPI adults don't support cutting agencies and want a focus on costs: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders do not agree with the strategy of putting entire federal agencies on the chopping block, nor are they broadly on board with mass layoffs of federal workers, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blizzard Entertainment president says 'there's a game for you' no matter what type of player you are
item.description: IRVINE, Calif. (AP) — California gaming giant Blizzard Entertainment announced Thursday that its popular event BlizzCon is coming back after several years off. The celebration of all things Blizzard, which will be held at the Anaheim Convention Center, is scheduled for September 2026.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4
